headline: The ASN.1 codec's universal-type metadata moves to a single descriptor registry.
summary: Internal refactor with no behavior or API change: the DER codec's universal-type table — each type's tag and whether DER requires it encoded primitive or constructed — is now defined once in a descriptor registry, and the tag constants plus the decoder's structural form checks are derived from it. This is the foundation for deriving the upcoming certificate/CRL/CMS format parsers from their ASN.1 grammar rather than hand-writing each.

Changed: The ASN.1/DER codec's universal-type metadata is now defined once in a descriptor registry (each entry carries the type's tag and its required DER encoding form). pki.asn1.TAGS, the primitive-only set (a type DER requires primitive, encoded constructed, is rejected) and the constructed-only set (a SEQUENCE/SET encoded primitive is rejected) are all derived from it, so registering a universal type is a single data entry. This is an internal refactor: the public surface and every decode/encode result are unchanged, and it lays the groundwork for schema-driven format parsers.
